text.title: Extended Thinking Rules and Patterns
text.summary: Rules for enabling and consuming Claude Extended Thinking: budget sizing, max_tokens constraint, multi-turn preservation, and usage monitoring.

rule.statement: Set max_tokens to at least budget_tokens + 2048 to avoid truncating the answer block.
rule.rationale: Thinking tokens count toward the max_tokens budget. If max_tokens equals budget_tokens, the answer block may be cut off mid-sentence with no error.

rule.statement: Start with budget_tokens=5000; increase only when answer quality is insufficient.
rule.rationale: More thinking budget does not always produce better answers. Start low and tune empirically to avoid unnecessary cost.

rule.statement: Do not set budget_tokens=0 to disable thinking silently; set thinking.type="disabled" instead.
rule.rationale: A budget of 0 disables thinking silently but the API still processes the thinking parameter, causing confusion. Use the explicit disable form.

rule.statement: Do not combine Extended Thinking with the temperature parameter.
rule.rationale: Setting both raises an API error. Extended Thinking requires deterministic reasoning; temperature is incompatible.

rule.statement: Do not combine Extended Thinking with streaming unless you can parse thinking SSE deltas.
rule.rationale: The event format for thinking deltas differs from text deltas. Mixing them without delta-aware parsing produces garbled output.

rule.statement: Preserve thinking blocks in the message history when continuing a multi-turn thinking conversation.
rule.rationale: Stripping thinking blocks from prior turns breaks the model's reasoning context, producing lower-quality responses in subsequent turns.

rule.statement: Never expose thinking blocks to end users.
rule.rationale: Thinking outputs can include the model reasoning about its own limitations and internal uncertainties; surfacing them to users undermines trust.

rule.statement: Track cache_read_input_tokens in usage to verify prompt caching is active when used alongside Extended Thinking.
rule.rationale: Thinking tokens are non-cacheable. Only the non-thinking input prefix can be cached. Monitor separately to understand actual savings.

example.code:
import anthropic client = anthropic.Anthropic()
def think_deeply(problem: str, budget: int = 8000) -> tuple[str, str]: resp = client.messages.create( model="claude-opus-4-5-20251101", max_tokens=budget + 4096, # must exceed budget_tokens thinking={"type": "enabled", "budget_tokens": budget}, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": problem}] ) thinking = next((b.thinking for b in resp.content if b.type == "thinking"), "") answer = next((b.text for b in resp.content if b.type == "text"), "") return thinking, answer
example.note: max_tokens is set to budget + 4096 to guarantee the answer block is never truncated.

text.reference: Extended Thinking helper returning (thinking, answer) tuple
